trending.title: New merger rules are no free ride for European champions, says Teresa Ribera
trending.body: The EU’s competition chief rejects consolidation-as-strategy argument ahead of next month’s unveiling of the revamped regulations.

trending.title: EU eyes tougher penalties for single market rule-breakers
trending.body: A draft joint declaration on One Europe, One Market Roadmap calls for Brussels to penalize foot-dragging by EU countries.

trending.title: EU says WhatsApp AI terms update fails to resolve antitrust concerns
trending.body: Commission finds that Meta’s proposal to charge fees for rival AI assistants on WhatsApp is “effectively equivalent” to an outright ban on access.

trending.title: EU hires former von der Leyen aide as top competition official
trending.body: Anthony Whelan fills key antitrust role after monthslong search.

trending.title: The EU’s Big Tech rulebook is shifting the digital economy, says Ribera
trending.body: Speaking at POLITICO’s European Pulse Forum, the EU’s top antitrust official pushed back against criticism that enforcement has been too slow.

competition.title: New merger rules are no free ride for European champions, says Teresa Ribera
competition.body: The EU’s competition chief rejects consolidation-as-strategy argument ahead of next month’s unveiling of the revamped regulations.

competition.title: EU eyes tougher penalties for single market rule-breakers
competition.body: A draft joint declaration on One Europe, One Market Roadmap calls for Brussels to penalize foot-dragging by EU countries.

competition.title: EU says WhatsApp AI terms update fails to resolve antitrust concerns
competition.body: Commission finds that Meta’s proposal to charge fees for rival AI assistants on WhatsApp is “effectively equivalent” to an outright ban on access.

competition.title: EU hires former von der Leyen aide as top competition official
competition.body: Anthony Whelan fills key antitrust role after monthslong search.

competition.title: The EU’s Big Tech rulebook is shifting the digital economy, says Ribera
competition.body: Speaking at POLITICO’s European Pulse Forum, the EU’s top antitrust official pushed back against criticism that enforcement has been too slow.

privacy.title: Don’t ‘overload’ US deal with extra demands, says EU trade boss
privacy.body: “A deal is a deal,” Maroš Šefčovič tells  POLITICO.

privacy.title: Germany’s digital minister wants a European Palantir
privacy.body: Karsten Wildberger tells POLITICO European governments should support homegrown startups in scaling up.

regulatory.title: UK intelligence: 100 nations have spyware that can hack Britain
regulatory.body: Security officials will warn there is an increasing cyber threat from nation states at a conference in Glasgow on Wednesday.

regulatory.title: White House meets with Anthropic CEO amid hopes for a truce
regulatory.body: The “introductory meeting” came as the Trump administration is considering how to deploy a groundbreaking new Anthropic model amid its feud with the leading AI company.

regulatory.title: Brussels launched an age checking app. Hackers say it takes 2 minutes to break it.
regulatory.body: Cyber experts say they have found holes in Brussels’ age verification app, despite claims by the EU executive that it is “technically ready.”

regulatory.title: Online age checks are coming in Europe
regulatory.body: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen says the world’s biggest tech platforms have “no more excuses” for not checking users’ ages before letting them access restricted content.

regulatory.title: ‘No more excuses’: Von der Leyen says EU age checking app is ready
regulatory.body: Age verification app is central to EU effort to keep kids safe online.

regulatory.title: European civil servants are being forced off WhatsApp
regulatory.body: Governments want to move away from “platforms over which we have no control,” says Dutch minister.

regulatory.title: Former von der Leyen aide’s new role reflects EU competition policy shift
regulatory.body: Veteran eurocrat Anthony Whelan is a safe pair of hands to lead the Commission’s competition department into a new era — especially for President Ursula von der Leyen.

regulatory.title: Robots captured Russian army positions for first time in history, Zelenskyy says
regulatory.body: “The occupiers surrendered, and the operation was carried out without infantry and without losses on our side,” says Ukrainian leader.

regulatory.title: European regulators sidelined on Anthropic superhacking model
regulatory.body: New AI tech could pose major cybersecurity risks but many European regulators have limited oversight.

regulatory.title: Germany’s €2B battlefield digitalization system runs into trouble
regulatory.body: Berlin is seeking fresh funding for a troubled comms system that raises questions about the country’s capacity to rearm.

regulatory.title: EU Parliament threatens to delay €1.8 trillion budget talks until 2027
regulatory.body: Lawmakers want to start technical negotiations only after member countries decide on the overall size of the EU cash pot.

regulatory.title: EU complaints system buckles under pressure of AI
regulatory.body: Regulators complain of surging workload and bottlenecks as citizens turn to AI to interact with officials.

regulatory.title: Europe should regulate Big Tech instead of banning kids from social media, Estonia says
regulatory.body: Banning kids from social media won’t work, as they “will find very quickly the ways to go around and to still use social media,” Estonian Education Minister Kristina Kallas said.

regulatory.title: UK looks to hold tech execs personally liable for intimate image abuse
regulatory.body: The U.K. government simultaneously announced it would ban porn depicting incest.

regulatory.title: 8 in 10 Europeans don’t trust US, Chinese firms with data
regulatory.body: Europe is rolling out measures to keep data local and reduce reliance on foreign tech.

regulatory.title: Orbán’s government accuses Facebook of undermining his reelection campaign
regulatory.body: The “algorithm is basically working against the government parties,” said the government spokesperson — a claim that Facebook denies.
